metadata.mechanics_review: The spell provides significant crowd control. The halving of speed, penalty to AC/Dex saves, restriction on actions (action OR bonus action), and reduction in attacks (max one attack) are standard but potent effects for a 3rd-level spell. The unique mechanic regarding 1-action spellcasting (requiring a d20 check and delaying the spell) adds a layer of tactical depth and potential for disruption. The concentration requirement and end-of-turn save throw are appropriate balancing factors.

consistency_check.level_vs_effect: Appropriate for 3rd level. It is powerful control, balanced by concentration and a save at the end of every turn.
consistency_check.school_consistency: Transmutation fits the theme of altering physical properties (speed, reaction time).
consistency_check.component_check: The material component (a drop of molasses) is flavorful and consistent with the theme of slowness.

quality_assessment.clarity: The description is very clear regarding all imposed limitations.
quality_assessment.mechanical_completeness: All mechanical effects (speed reduction, penalties, action economy restrictions, spellcasting delay) are explicitly defined.

mechanics_evaluation.saving_throw: Wisdom save, which is typical for spells affecting mental state/time perception.
mechanics_evaluation.action_economy_impact: Severe. Halving speed, limiting actions to one (action OR bonus action), and capping attacks to one per turn creates massive debuffs.
mechanics_evaluation.spellcasting_delay: The d20 check for 1-action spells is the most unique and potentially frustrating mechanic for the target, effectively guaranteeing a loss of one full turn's action economy on a failed save if they attempt to cast a leveled spell.

llm_audit.suggested_updates: None required. The spell is well-defined and mechanically sound as written for its intended tier of play.
